{{Infobox ancient site | name = Takarazuka Kofun | native_name =宝塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Izumo, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|''' Takarazuka Kofun'''|宝塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Shimogoshi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Izumo, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139124|title= 宝塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period. It is also called the {{nihongo|''' Ippo-zuka'''|一保塚|}}.
==Overview== The Takarazuka Kofun is located on the natural embankment of the Kando River, surrounded by [[paddy field]]s and 100 meters east of Izumonishi High School. Much of the tumulus has been lost, to the extent that the ceiling stone of the [[burial chamber]] is exposed, so the original shape and size of the tumulus is uncertain. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the vicinity. The burial chamber has an opening to the south, and is 3.6 meters deep, 2.1 meters wide, and 2.5 meters high. Cut [[tuff]] stone is used for the wall stones, the back wall is a single stone, the east wall of the side wall is two-tiered with large stones in the lower part, and the west wall is two-tiered with large stones in the back, while the front side is a stone wall. It contains a house-shaped sarcophagus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, so only a few iron arrowheads have been recovered as [[grave goods]]. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, judging from the structure of the stone chamber.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about four kilometers (ten minutes by car) from [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139124|title= 宝塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the vicinity. The burial chamber has an opening to the south, and is 3.6 meters deep, 2.1 meters wide, and 2.5 meters high. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takarazuka Kofun | native_name =宝塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Izumo, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|''' Takarazuka Kofun'''|宝塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Shimogoshi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Izumo, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139124|title= 宝塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period. It is also called the {{nihongo|''' Ippo-zuka'''|一保塚|}}.
==Overview== The Takarazuka Kofun is located on the natural embankment of the Kando River, surrounded by [[paddy field]]s and 100 meters east of Izumonishi High School. Much of the tumulus has been lost, to the extent that the ceiling stone of the [[burial chamber]] is exposed, so the original shape and size of the tumulus is uncertain. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the vicinity. The burial chamber has an opening to the south, and is 3.6 meters deep, 2.1 meters wide, and 2.5 meters high. Cut [[tuff]] stone is used for the wall stones, the back wall is a single stone, the east wall of the side wall is two-tiered with large stones in the lower part, and the west wall is two-tiered with large stones in the back, while the front side is a stone wall. It contains a house-shaped sarcophagus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, so only a few iron arrowheads have been recovered as [[grave goods]]. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, judging from the structure of the stone chamber.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about four kilometers (ten minutes by car) from [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Takarazuka Kofun | native_name =宝塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Izumo, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|''' Takarazuka Kofun'''|宝塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Shimogoshi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Izumo, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139124|title= 宝塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period. It is also called the {{nihongo|''' Ippo-zuka'''|一保塚|}}.
==Overview== The Takarazuka Kofun is located on the natural embankment of the Kando River, surrounded by [[paddy field]]s and 100 meters east of Izumonishi High School. Much of the tumulus has been lost, to the extent that the ceiling stone of the [[burial chamber]] is exposed, so the original shape and size of the tumulus is uncertain. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the vicinity. The burial chamber has an opening to the south, and is 3.6 meters deep, 2.1 meters wide, and 2.5 meters high. Cut [[tuff]] stone is used for the wall stones, the back wall is a single stone, the east wall of the side wall is two-tiered with large stones in the lower part, and the west wall is two-tiered with large stones in the back, while the front side is a stone wall. It contains a house-shaped sarcophagus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, so only a few iron arrowheads have been recovered as [[grave goods]]. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, judging from the structure of the stone chamber.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about four kilometers (ten minutes by car) from [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://iseki.shimane-u.ac.jp/search_detail.php?target=site&org_target=site&subtarget=literature&id=5632&search_kbn=site&page=1&act=&sitename=温湯城&sitekana= Archaeology Database of Shimane]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Takarazuka Kofun | native_name =宝塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Izumo, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|''' Takarazuka Kofun'''|宝塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Shimogoshi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Izumo, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139124|title= 宝塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period. It is also called the {{nihongo|''' Ippo-zuka'''|一保塚|}}.
==Overview== The Takarazuka Kofun is located on the natural embankment of the Kando River, surrounded by [[paddy field]]s and 100 meters east of Izumonishi High School. Much of the tumulus has been lost, to the extent that the ceiling stone of the [[burial chamber]] is exposed, so the original shape and size of the tumulus is uncertain. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the vicinity. The burial chamber has an opening to the south, and is 3.6 meters deep, 2.1 meters wide, and 2.5 meters high. Cut [[tuff]] stone is used for the wall stones, the back wall is a single stone, the east wall of the side wall is two-tiered with large stones in the lower part, and the west wall is two-tiered with large stones in the back, while the front side is a stone wall. It contains a house-shaped sarcophagus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, so only a few iron arrowheads have been recovered as [[grave goods]]. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, judging from the structure of the stone chamber.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about four kilometers (ten minutes by car) from [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Takarazuka Kofun | native_name =宝塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Izumo, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|''' Takarazuka Kofun'''|宝塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Shimogoshi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Izumo, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139124|title= 宝塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period. It is also called the {{nihongo|''' Ippo-zuka'''|一保塚|}}.
==Overview== The Takarazuka Kofun is located on the natural embankment of the Kando River, surrounded by [[paddy field]]s and 100 meters east of Izumonishi High School. Much of the tumulus has been lost, to the extent that the ceiling stone of the [[burial chamber]] is exposed, so the original shape and size of the tumulus is uncertain. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the vicinity. The burial chamber has an opening to the south, and is 3.6 meters deep, 2.1 meters wide, and 2.5 meters high. Cut [[tuff]] stone is used for the wall stones, the back wall is a single stone, the east wall of the side wall is two-tiered with large stones in the lower part, and the west wall is two-tiered with large stones in the back, while the front side is a stone wall. It contains a house-shaped sarcophagus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, so only a few iron arrowheads have been recovered as [[grave goods]]. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, judging from the structure of the stone chamber.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about four kilometers (ten minutes by car) from [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takarazuka Kofun (Izumo)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Takarazuka Kofun (Izumo)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Takarazuka Kofun | native_name =宝塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Izumo, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|''' Takarazuka Kofun'''|宝塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Shimogoshi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Izumo, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139124|title= 宝塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period. It is also called the {{nihongo|''' Ippo-zuka'''|一保塚|}}.
==Overview== The Takarazuka Kofun is located on the natural embankment of the Kando River, surrounded by [[paddy field]]s and 100 meters east of Izumonishi High School. Much of the tumulus has been lost, to the extent that the ceiling stone of the [[burial chamber]] is exposed, so the original shape and size of the tumulus is uncertain. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the vicinity. The burial chamber has an opening to the south, and is 3.6 meters deep, 2.1 meters wide, and 2.5 meters high. Cut [[tuff]] stone is used for the wall stones, the back wall is a single stone, the east wall of the side wall is two-tiered with large stones in the lower part, and the west wall is two-tiered with large stones in the back, while the front side is a stone wall. It contains a house-shaped sarcophagus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, so only a few iron arrowheads have been recovered as [[grave goods]]. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, judging from the structure of the stone chamber.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about four kilometers (ten minutes by car) from [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

Cut [[tuff]] stone is used for the wall stones, the back wall is a single stone, the east wall of the side wall is two-tiered with large stones in the lower part, and the west wall is two-tiered with large stones in the back, while the front side is a stone wall. It contains a house-shaped sarcophagus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, so only a few iron arrowheads have been recovered as [[grave goods]]. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, judging from the structure of the stone chamber.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about four kilometers (ten minutes by car) from [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Takarazuka Kofun | native_name =宝塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Izumo, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|''' Takarazuka Kofun'''|宝塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Shimogoshi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Izumo, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139124|title= 宝塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period. It is also called the {{nihongo|''' Ippo-zuka'''|一保塚|}}.
==Overview== The Takarazuka Kofun is located on the natural embankment of the Kando River, surrounded by [[paddy field]]s and 100 meters east of Izumonishi High School. Much of the tumulus has been lost, to the extent that the ceiling stone of the [[burial chamber]] is exposed, so the original shape and size of the tumulus is uncertain. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the vicinity. The burial chamber has an opening to the south, and is 3.6 meters deep, 2.1 meters wide, and 2.5 meters high. Cut [[tuff]] stone is used for the wall stones, the back wall is a single stone, the east wall of the side wall is two-tiered with large stones in the lower part, and the west wall is two-tiered with large stones in the back, while the front side is a stone wall. It contains a house-shaped sarcophagus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, so only a few iron arrowheads have been recovered as [[grave goods]]. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, judging from the structure of the stone chamber.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about four kilometers (ten minutes by car) from [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|''' Takarazuka Kofun'''|宝塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Shimogoshi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Izumo, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139124|title= 宝塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period. It is also called the {{nihongo|''' Ippo-zuka'''|一保塚|}}.
==Overview== The Takarazuka Kofun is located on the natural embankment of the Kando River, surrounded by [[paddy field]]s and 100 meters east of Izumonishi High School. Much of the tumulus has been lost, to the extent that the ceiling stone of the [[burial chamber]] is exposed, so the original shape and size of the tumulus is uncertain. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the vicinity. The burial chamber has an opening to the south, and is 3.6 meters deep, 2.1 meters wide, and 2.5 meters high. Cut [[tuff]] stone is used for the wall stones, the back wall is a single stone, the east wall of the side wall is two-tiered with large stones in the lower part, and the west wall is two-tiered with large stones in the back, while the front side is a stone wall. It contains a house-shaped sarcophagus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, so only a few iron arrowheads have been recovered as [[grave goods]]. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, judging from the structure of the stone chamber.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about four kilometers (ten minutes by car) from [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

The {{nihongo|''' Takarazuka Kofun'''|宝塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Shimogoshi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Izumo, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139124|title= 宝塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period. It is also called the {{nihongo|''' Ippo-zuka'''|一保塚|}}.
==Overview== The Takarazuka Kofun is located on the natural embankment of the Kando River, surrounded by [[paddy field]]s and 100 meters east of Izumonishi High School. Much of the tumulus has been lost, to the extent that the ceiling stone of the [[burial chamber]] is exposed, so the original shape and size of the tumulus is uncertain. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the vicinity. The burial chamber has an opening to the south, and is 3.6 meters deep, 2.1 meters wide, and 2.5 meters high. Cut [[tuff]] stone is used for the wall stones, the back wall is a single stone, the east wall of the side wall is two-tiered with large stones in the lower part, and the west wall is two-tiered with large stones in the back, while the front side is a stone wall. It contains a house-shaped sarcophagus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, so only a few iron arrowheads have been recovered as [[grave goods]]. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, judging from the structure of the stone chamber.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about four kilometers (ten minutes by car) from [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Takarazuka Kofun | native_name =宝塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Izumo, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|''' Takarazuka Kofun'''|宝塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Shimogoshi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Izumo, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139124|title= 宝塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period. It is also called the {{nihongo|''' Ippo-zuka'''|一保塚|}}.
==Overview== The Takarazuka Kofun is located on the natural embankment of the Kando River, surrounded by [[paddy field]]s and 100 meters east of Izumonishi High School. Much of the tumulus has been lost, to the extent that the ceiling stone of the [[burial chamber]] is exposed, so the original shape and size of the tumulus is uncertain. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the vicinity. The burial chamber has an opening to the south, and is 3.6 meters deep, 2.1 meters wide, and 2.5 meters high. Cut [[tuff]] stone is used for the wall stones, the back wall is a single stone, the east wall of the side wall is two-tiered with large stones in the lower part, and the west wall is two-tiered with large stones in the back, while the front side is a stone wall. It contains a house-shaped sarcophagus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, so only a few iron arrowheads have been recovered as [[grave goods]]. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, judging from the structure of the stone chamber.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about four kilometers (ten minutes by car) from [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139124|title= 宝塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period. It is also called the {{nihongo|''' Ippo-zuka'''|一保塚|}}.
==Overview== The Takarazuka Kofun is located on the natural embankment of the Kando River, surrounded by [[paddy field]]s and 100 meters east of Izumonishi High School. Much of the tumulus has been lost, to the extent that the ceiling stone of the [[burial chamber]] is exposed, so the original shape and size of the tumulus is uncertain. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the vicinity. The burial chamber has an opening to the south, and is 3.6 meters deep, 2.1 meters wide, and 2.5 meters high. Cut [[tuff]] stone is used for the wall stones, the back wall is a single stone, the east wall of the side wall is two-tiered with large stones in the lower part, and the west wall is two-tiered with large stones in the back, while the front side is a stone wall. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takarazuka Kofun | native_name =宝塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Izumo, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|''' Takarazuka Kofun'''|宝塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Shimogoshi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Izumo, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139124|title= 宝塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period. It is also called the {{nihongo|''' Ippo-zuka'''|一保塚|}}.
==Overview== The Takarazuka Kofun is located on the natural embankment of the Kando River, surrounded by [[paddy field]]s and 100 meters east of Izumonishi High School. Much of the tumulus has been lost, to the extent that the ceiling stone of the [[burial chamber]] is exposed, so the original shape and size of the tumulus is uncertain. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the vicinity. The burial chamber has an opening to the south, and is 3.6 meters deep, 2.1 meters wide, and 2.5 meters high. Cut [[tuff]] stone is used for the wall stones, the back wall is a single stone, the east wall of the side wall is two-tiered with large stones in the lower part, and the west wall is two-tiered with large stones in the back, while the front side is a stone wall. It contains a house-shaped sarcophagus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, so only a few iron arrowheads have been recovered as [[grave goods]]. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, judging from the structure of the stone chamber.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about four kilometers (ten minutes by car) from [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Takarazuka Kofun | native_name =宝塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Izumo, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|''' Takarazuka Kofun'''|宝塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Shimogoshi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Izumo, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139124|title= 宝塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period. It is also called the {{nihongo|''' Ippo-zuka'''|一保塚|}}.
==Overview== The Takarazuka Kofun is located on the natural embankment of the Kando River, surrounded by [[paddy field]]s and 100 meters east of Izumonishi High School. Much of the tumulus has been lost, to the extent that the ceiling stone of the [[burial chamber]] is exposed, so the original shape and size of the tumulus is uncertain. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the vicinity. The burial chamber has an opening to the south, and is 3.6 meters deep, 2.1 meters wide, and 2.5 meters high. Cut [[tuff]] stone is used for the wall stones, the back wall is a single stone, the east wall of the side wall is two-tiered with large stones in the lower part, and the west wall is two-tiered with large stones in the back, while the front side is a stone wall. It contains a house-shaped sarcophagus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, so only a few iron arrowheads have been recovered as [[grave goods]]. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, judging from the structure of the stone chamber.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about four kilometers (ten minutes by car) from [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139124|title= 宝塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the vicinity. The burial chamber has an opening to the south, and is 3.6 meters deep, 2.1 meters wide, and 2.5 meters high. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takarazuka Kofun | native_name =宝塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Izumo, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|''' Takarazuka Kofun'''|宝塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Shimogoshi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Izumo, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139124|title= 宝塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period. It is also called the {{nihongo|''' Ippo-zuka'''|一保塚|}}.
==Overview== The Takarazuka Kofun is located on the natural embankment of the Kando River, surrounded by [[paddy field]]s and 100 meters east of Izumonishi High School. Much of the tumulus has been lost, to the extent that the ceiling stone of the [[burial chamber]] is exposed, so the original shape and size of the tumulus is uncertain. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the vicinity. The burial chamber has an opening to the south, and is 3.6 meters deep, 2.1 meters wide, and 2.5 meters high. Cut [[tuff]] stone is used for the wall stones, the back wall is a single stone, the east wall of the side wall is two-tiered with large stones in the lower part, and the west wall is two-tiered with large stones in the back, while the front side is a stone wall. It contains a house-shaped sarcophagus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, so only a few iron arrowheads have been recovered as [[grave goods]]. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, judging from the structure of the stone chamber.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about four kilometers (ten minutes by car) from [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Takarazuka Kofun | native_name =宝塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Izumo, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|''' Takarazuka Kofun'''|宝塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Shimogoshi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Izumo, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139124|title= 宝塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period. It is also called the {{nihongo|''' Ippo-zuka'''|一保塚|}}.
==Overview== The Takarazuka Kofun is located on the natural embankment of the Kando River, surrounded by [[paddy field]]s and 100 meters east of Izumonishi High School. Much of the tumulus has been lost, to the extent that the ceiling stone of the [[burial chamber]] is exposed, so the original shape and size of the tumulus is uncertain. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the vicinity. The burial chamber has an opening to the south, and is 3.6 meters deep, 2.1 meters wide, and 2.5 meters high. Cut [[tuff]] stone is used for the wall stones, the back wall is a single stone, the east wall of the side wall is two-tiered with large stones in the lower part, and the west wall is two-tiered with large stones in the back, while the front side is a stone wall. It contains a house-shaped sarcophagus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, so only a few iron arrowheads have been recovered as [[grave goods]]. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, judging from the structure of the stone chamber.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about four kilometers (ten minutes by car) from [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Takarazuka Kofun | native_name =宝塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Izumo, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|''' Takarazuka Kofun'''|宝塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Shimogoshi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Izumo, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139124|title= 宝塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period. It is also called the {{nihongo|''' Ippo-zuka'''|一保塚|}}.
==Overview== The Takarazuka Kofun is located on the natural embankment of the Kando River, surrounded by [[paddy field]]s and 100 meters east of Izumonishi High School. Much of the tumulus has been lost, to the extent that the ceiling stone of the [[burial chamber]] is exposed, so the original shape and size of the tumulus is uncertain. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the vicinity. The burial chamber has an opening to the south, and is 3.6 meters deep, 2.1 meters wide, and 2.5 meters high. Cut [[tuff]] stone is used for the wall stones, the back wall is a single stone, the east wall of the side wall is two-tiered with large stones in the lower part, and the west wall is two-tiered with large stones in the back, while the front side is a stone wall. It contains a house-shaped sarcophagus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, so only a few iron arrowheads have been recovered as [[grave goods]]. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, judging from the structure of the stone chamber.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about four kilometers (ten minutes by car) from [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://iseki.shimane-u.ac.jp/search_detail.php?target=site&org_target=site&subtarget=literature&id=5632&search_kbn=site&page=1&act=&sitename=温湯城&sitekana= Archaeology Database of {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takarazuka Kofun | native_name =宝塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Izumo, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|''' Takarazuka Kofun'''|宝塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Shimogoshi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Izumo, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139124|title= 宝塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period. It is also called the {{nihongo|''' Ippo-zuka'''|一保塚|}}.
==Overview== The Takarazuka Kofun is located on the natural embankment of the Kando River, surrounded by [[paddy field]]s and 100 meters east of Izumonishi High School. Much of the tumulus has been lost, to the extent that the ceiling stone of the [[burial chamber]] is exposed, so the original shape and size of the tumulus is uncertain. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the vicinity. The burial chamber has an opening to the south, and is 3.6 meters deep, 2.1 meters wide, and 2.5 meters high. Cut [[tuff]] stone is used for the wall stones, the back wall is a single stone, the east wall of the side wall is two-tiered with large stones in the lower part, and the west wall is two-tiered with large stones in the back, while the front side is a stone wall. It contains a house-shaped sarcophagus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, so only a few iron arrowheads have been recovered as [[grave goods]]. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, judging from the structure of the stone chamber.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about four kilometers (ten minutes by car) from [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|''' Takarazuka Kofun'''|宝塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Shimogoshi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is also called the {{nihongo|''' Ippo-zuka'''|一保塚|}}.
==Overview== The Takarazuka Kofun is located on the natural embankment of the Kando River, surrounded by [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s and 100 meters east of Izumonishi High School. Much of the tumulus has been lost, to the extent that the ceiling stone of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is exposed, so the original shape and size of the tumulus is uncertain. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found in the vicinity. The burial chamber has an opening to the south, and is 3.6 meters deep, 2.1 meters wide, and 2.5 meters high. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Takarazuka Kofun (Izumo)]][[en:Takarazuka Kofun (Izumo)]][[ja:宝塚古墳 (出雲市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q122333002]] Wikidata name: Takarazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takarazuka Kofun | native_name =宝塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image =File:Takarazuka Kofun (Izumo), funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.33916666666667|N|132.73333333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Izumo|WD=Q825166}} | region = [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|''' Takarazuka Kofun'''|宝塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Shimogoshi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139124|title= 宝塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period. It is also called the {{nihongo|''' Ippo-zuka'''|一保塚|}}.
==Overview== The Takarazuka Kofun is located on the natural embankment of the Kando River, surrounded by [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s and 100 meters east of Izumonishi High School. Much of the tumulus has been lost, to the extent that the ceiling stone of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is exposed, so the original shape and size of the tumulus is uncertain. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found in the vicinity. The burial chamber has an opening to the south, and is 3.6 meters deep, 2.1 meters wide, and 2.5 meters high. Cut [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] stone is used for the wall stones, the back wall is a single stone, the east wall of the side wall is two-tiered with large stones in the lower part, and the west wall is two-tiered with large stones in the back, while the front side is a stone wall. It contains a house-shaped sarcophagus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, so only a few iron arrowheads have been recovered as [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, judging from the structure of the stone chamber.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about four kilometers (ten minutes by car) from [[:en:Izumoshi Station|Izumoshi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Takarazuka Kofun (Izumo)]][[en:Takarazuka Kofun (Izumo)]][[ja:宝塚古墳 (出雲市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q122333002]] Wikidata name: Takarazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takarazuka Kofun | native_name =宝塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image =File:Takarazuka Kofun (Izumo), funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.33916666666667|N|132.73333333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Izumo|WD=Q825166}} | region = [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|''' Takarazuka Kofun'''|宝塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Shimogoshi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139124|title= 宝塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period. It is also called the {{nihongo|''' Ippo-zuka'''|一保塚|}}.
==Overview== The Takarazuka Kofun is located on the natural embankment of the Kando River, surrounded by [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s and 100 meters east of Izumonishi High School. Much of the tumulus has been lost, to the extent that the ceiling stone of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is exposed, so the original shape and size of the tumulus is uncertain. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found in the vicinity. The burial chamber has an opening to the south, and is 3.6 meters deep, 2.1 meters wide, and 2.5 meters high. Cut [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] stone is used for the wall stones, the back wall is a single stone, the east wall of the side wall is two-tiered with large stones in the lower part, and the west wall is two-tiered with large stones in the back, while the front side is a stone wall. It contains a house-shaped sarcophagus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, so only a few iron arrowheads have been recovered as [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, judging from the structure of the stone chamber.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about four kilometers (ten minutes by car) from [[:en:Izumoshi Station|Izumoshi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

The {{nihongo|''' Takarazuka Kofun'''|宝塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Shimogoshi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139124|title= 宝塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period. It is also called the {{nihongo|''' Ippo-zuka'''|一保塚|}}.
==Overview== The Takarazuka Kofun is located on the natural embankment of the Kando River, surrounded by [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s and 100 meters east of Izumonishi High School. Much of the tumulus has been lost, to the extent that the ceiling stone of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is exposed, so the original shape and size of the tumulus is uncertain. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found in the vicinity. The burial chamber has an opening to the south, and is 3.6 meters deep, 2.1 meters wide, and 2.5 meters high. Cut [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] stone is used for the wall stones, the back wall is a single stone, the east wall of the side wall is two-tiered with large stones in the lower part, and the west wall is two-tiered with large stones in the back, while the front side is a stone wall. It contains a house-shaped sarcophagus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, so only a few iron arrowheads have been recovered as [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, judging from the structure of the stone chamber.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about four kilometers (ten minutes by car) from [[:en:Izumoshi Station|Izumoshi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It contains a house-shaped sarcophagus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, so only a few iron arrowheads have been recovered as {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, judging from the structure of the stone chamber.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about four kilometers (ten minutes by car) from {{ill|Izumoshi Station|en|Izumoshi Station|WD=Q860288|lt=Izumoshi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Takarazuka Kofun (Izumo)]][[en:Takarazuka Kofun (Izumo)]][[ja:宝塚古墳 (出雲市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Takarazuka Kofun (Izumo)]][[en:Takarazuka Kofun (Izumo)]][[ja:宝塚古墳 (出雲市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Takarazuka Kofun (Izumo)]][[en:Takarazuka Kofun (Izumo)]][[ja:宝塚古墳 (出雲市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q122333002]] Wikidata name: Takarazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takarazuka Kofun | native_name =宝塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image =File:Takarazuka Kofun (Izumo), funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.33916666666667|N|132.73333333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Izumo|WD=Q825166}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|''' Takarazuka Kofun'''|宝塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Shimogoshi-cho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Izumo|en|Izumo|WD=Q301580|lt=Izumo}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139124|title= 宝塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period. It is also called the {{nihongo|''' Ippo-zuka'''|一保塚|}}.
==Overview== The Takarazuka Kofun is located on the natural embankment of the Kando River, surrounded by {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=Paddy field}}s and 100 meters east of Izumonishi High School. Much of the tumulus has been lost, to the extent that the ceiling stone of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is exposed, so the original shape and size of the tumulus is uncertain. Fragments of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found in the vicinity. The burial chamber has an opening to the south, and is 3.6 meters deep, 2.1 meters wide, and 2.5 meters high. Cut {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} stone is used for the wall stones, the back wall is a single stone, the east wall of the side wall is two-tiered with large stones in the lower part, and the west wall is two-tiered with large stones in the back, while the front side is a stone wall. It contains a house-shaped sarcophagus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, so only a few iron arrowheads have been recovered as {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, judging from the structure of the stone chamber.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about four kilometers (ten minutes by car) from {{ill|Izumoshi Station|en|Izumoshi Station|WD=Q860288|lt=Izumoshi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://iseki.shimane-u.ac.jp/search_detail.php?target=site&org_target=site&subtarget=literature&id=5632&search_kbn=site&page=1&act=&sitename=温湯城&sitekana= Archaeology Database of Shimane]{{in lang|ja}} Takarazuka Kofun (Izumo)

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Takarazuka Kofun | native_name =宝塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image =File:Takarazuka Kofun (Izumo), funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.33916666666667|N|132.73333333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Izumo|WD=Q825166}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|21|N|132|44|00|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|''' Takarazuka Kofun'''|宝塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Shimogoshi-cho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Izumo|en|Izumo|WD=Q301580|lt=Izumo}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139124|title= 宝塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period. It is also called the {{nihongo|''' Ippo-zuka'''|一保塚|}}.
==Overview== The Takarazuka Kofun is located on the natural embankment of the Kando River, surrounded by {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=Paddy field}}s and 100 meters east of Izumonishi High School. Much of the tumulus has been lost, to the extent that the ceiling stone of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is exposed, so the original shape and size of the tumulus is uncertain. Fragments of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found in the vicinity. The burial chamber has an opening to the south, and is 3.6 meters deep, 2.1 meters wide, and 2.5 meters high. Cut {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} stone is used for the wall stones, the back wall is a single stone, the east wall of the side wall is two-tiered with large stones in the lower part, and the west wall is two-tiered with large stones in the back, while the front side is a stone wall. It contains a house-shaped sarcophagus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, so only a few iron arrowheads have been recovered as {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, judging from the structure of the stone chamber.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about four kilometers (ten minutes by car) from {{ill|Izumoshi Station|en|Izumoshi Station|WD=Q860288|lt=Izumoshi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://iseki.shimane-u.ac.jp/search_detail.php?target=site&org_target=site&subtarget=literature&id=5632&search_kbn=site&page=1&act=&sitename=温湯城&sitekana= Archaeology Database of Shimane]{{in lang|ja}} Takarazuka Kofun (Izumo)
{{Kofun navbar}}

The {{nihongo|''' Takarazuka Kofun'''|宝塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Shimogoshi-cho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Izumo|en|Izumo|WD=Q301580|lt=Izumo|1=Izumo}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139124|title= 宝塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period. It is also called the {{nihongo|''' Ippo-zuka'''|一保塚|}}.
==Overview== The Takarazuka Kofun is located on the natural embankment of the Kando River, surrounded by {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=Paddy field|1=Paddy field}}s and 100 meters east of Izumonishi High School. Much of the tumulus has been lost, to the extent that the ceiling stone of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is exposed, so the original shape and size of the tumulus is uncertain. Fragments of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found in the vicinity. The burial chamber has an opening to the south, and is 3.6 meters deep, 2.1 meters wide, and 2.5 meters high. Cut {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} stone is used for the wall stones, the back wall is a single stone, the east wall of the side wall is two-tiered with large stones in the lower part, and the west wall is two-tiered with large stones in the back, while the front side is a stone wall. It contains a house-shaped sarcophagus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, so only a few iron arrowheads have been recovered as {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}. It is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, judging from the structure of the stone chamber.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about four kilometers (ten minutes by car) from {{ill|Izumoshi Station|en|Izumoshi Station|WD=Q860288|lt=Izumoshi Station|1=Izumoshi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://iseki.shimane-u.ac.jp/search_detail.php?target=site&org_target=site&subtarget=literature&id=5632&search_kbn=site&page=1&act=&sitename=温湯城&sitekana= Archaeology Database of Shimane]{{in lang|ja}} Takarazuka Kofun (Izumo)
